perturbation.original_text: 12. All disputes which may arise from or in connection with this Agreement shall be settled through negotiations between the Parties. If such a dispute between the Parties cannot be settled through negotiations between the Parties, either Party may submit such a dispute to a court in accordance with [ specify the jurisdiction ] laws.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. All disputes which may arise from or in connection with this Agreement shall be settled through negotiations between the Parties.
perturbation.explanation: The section regarding dispute resolution has been modified by removing the availability to submit a dispute to court. The new clause indicates that negotiation is the sole resolution method.
perturbation.law_explanation: By removing the option of court submission, the agreement might be interpreted as mandating arbitration, even if not explicitly stated. If the jurisdiction specified has laws favoring arbitration, this could unintentionally force arbitration, potentially conflicting with the FAA if the agreement doesn't meet its requirements for a valid arbitration agreement, or if the parties did not agree to arbitration.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate U.S. Code Notes prev | next A written provision in any maritime transaction or a contract evidencing a transaction involving commerce to settle by arbitration a controversy thereafter arising out of such contract or transaction, or the refusal to perform the whole or any part thereof, or an agreement in writing to submit to arbitration an existing controversy arising out of such a contract, transaction, or refusal, shall be valid, irrevocable, and enforceable, save upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity for the revocation of any contract or as otherwise provided in chapter 4. (July 30, 1947, ch. 392, 61 Stat. 670 ; Pub. L. 117–90, § 2(b)(1)(A) , Mar. 3, 2022 , 136 Stat. 27 .) Derivation Act Feb. 12, 1925, ch. 213, § 2, 43 Stat. 883 . Editorial Notes Amendments 2022— Pub. L. 117–90 inserted “or as otherwise provided in chapter 4” before period at end. Statutory Notes and Related Subsidiaries Effective Date of 2022 Amendment Amendment by Pub. L. 117–90 applicable with respect to any dispute or claim that arises or accrues on or after Mar. 3, 2022 , see section 3 of Pub. L. 117–90 , set out as an Effective Date note under section 401 of this title . U.S. Code Toolbox Law about... perturbation.justification: The original text allows for disputes to be submitted to court, while the changed text removes this option, limiting dispute resolution to negotiation only. The LLM explanation suggests this removal could imply mandatory arbitration, which may conflict with the FAA if the agreement doesn't meet arbitration agreement requirements. Scraped Law Snippet 1 and 2 (9 USC 2) both emphasize the validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate. While the changed text doesn't explicitly mandate arbitration, the removal of the court submission option, combined with the legal emphasis on arbitration agreements, creates a structural flaw that could unintentionally force arbitration without proper agreement, creating a legal contradiction as described under the structural flaws definition.

perturbation.original_text: 13. In case the Receiving Party breaches this Agreement , the Disclosing Party, the Target or any of their Affiliates are entitled to receive from the R eceiving Party compensation of financial and moral damages, suffered by the Disclosing Party, th e Target or any of their Affiliates respectively, as a result of such breach by the Rec eiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. In case the Receiving Party breaches this Agreement, the Disclosing Party is entitled to receive from the Receiving Party compensation of financial and moral damages, suffered by the Disclosing Party, as a result of such breach by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: I removed the Target or any of their affiliates, which will make it harder to bring damages from them.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Third Party Beneficiary Law - varies by state (e.g., California Civil Code § 1559)
perturbation.law_explanation: By removing the Target and its affiliates from the clause allowing them to receive damages for breach, it potentially eliminates their rights as third-party beneficiaries to the contract. This contradicts the principles of third-party beneficiary law, which grants rights to parties intended to benefit from a contract, even if they aren't direct signatories. The citation is an example since third party laws vary by jurisdiction.

OBLIGATIONS [1427 - 3273.69] ( Heading of Division 3 amended by Stats. 1988, Ch. 160, Sec. 14. ) PART 2. CONTRACTS [1549 - 1701] ( Part 2 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 1. NATURE OF A CONTRACT [1549 - 1615] ( Title 1 enacted 1872. ) CHAPTER 2. Parties [1556 - 1559] ( Chapter 2 enacted 1872. ) 1559. A contract, made expressly for the benefit of a third person, may be enforced by him at any time before the parties thereto rescind it. (Enacted 1872.)
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the Target and its affiliates to receive compensation for damages resulting from a breach by the Receiving Party. The changed text removes this right, potentially eliminating their ability to claim damages as third-party beneficiaries. Scraped Law Snippet 1 (California Code, CIV 1559) states that 'A contract, made expressly for the benefit of a third person, may be enforced by him at any time before the parties thereto rescind it.' By removing the Target and its affiliates, the modified contract potentially contradicts this law by hindering their ability to enforce the contract's benefits intended for them, thus creating a direct conflict with the principles of third-party beneficiary rights.
